milestone: Text Experience Words/Content — Manual Line Breaks Now Actually Render
slice: A real, user-reported gap: "in the builder when putting up text experience, enter should put new line in the experience." Pressing Enter in the Words/Content textarea already inserted a real newline -- the gap was downstream, in rendering: engineRuntime.js's _wrapLines split only on spaces, silently absorbing an embedded newline with zero visible break; slideRenderer.js's _layerDrawText (the Studio/Publish-side renderer for a compiled Text Experience) was worse -- a single unwrapped fillText call, no line breaks at all once Published. Fixed in lockstep: _wrapLines and _wrapText (slideRenderer.js) now split on newline into paragraphs first, then word-wrap each independently -- byte-identical to the old algorithm for any text with no newline at all. _layerDrawText was rewritten from one fillText call into a real multi-line renderer, reusing the fixed _wrapText for word-wrap-by-rect.w, with the existing anchor/align/drag-override/bbox math generalized from one line to N. Disclosed, deliberately out of scope: convergeSceneLayer's compiled text entry always hardcodes anchor:'top-left', silently discarding a Scene Layer's own chosen alignment -- a separate, pre-existing gap found while tracing this, not fixed here. Verified via a new Playwright suite: World Builder's real EngineV2Runtime.load/paintLayer renders a manual newline as 2 real fillText calls at distinct Y positions with zero regression for ordinary single-paragraph text; Studio's real SlideRenderer.buildPayload/render(), driven through a real compiled Layer Pack type:'text' entry, confirms the same newline now renders as 2 real lines instead of one squashed line. Both goldenBuild.js suites pass unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
